# Hook and CTA Swipefile

## Hook formulas

### Problem-first

```text
This is why people swipe in the first 2 seconds.
This video loses people before the tip starts.
Your video is not too slow. Your first payoff is too late.
Your captions are readable, but still in the wrong place.
```

### Result-first

```text
This one caption move makes the whole clip clearer.
Same clip. Two edits. Different result.
Here is the fixed version before I explain it.
This first-frame change makes the video easier to watch.
```

### Contrarian

```text
Captions are not enough. Placement matters more.
Posting time is not your growth strategy.
Fast cuts are not pacing.
Views are not the metric to optimise first.
```

### Proof-first

```text
Here is the retention drop from this edit.
This is where viewers leave.
I changed only the first frame. Watch the difference.
The graph changed after one visual fix.
```

### Experiment

```text
I tested 3 hooks. One failed instantly.
I tried 5 caption styles on the same clip.
I posted the same idea three ways.
I tested result-first against problem-first.
```

### Identity

```text
Every editor has made this mistake.
Send this to someone who puts captions over faces.
If you post Shorts/Reels/TikToks, check this before editing.
Small creators keep missing this retention leak.
```

## CTA formulas

### Save

```text
Save this before editing your next Reel.
Save this caption placement checklist.
Save this as your pre-post retention check.
Save the 4-line hook formula.
```

### Send

```text
Send this to someone whose captions cover the subject.
Send this to the friend editing your clips.
Send this to someone still using #FYP as a strategy.
Send this to a creator who opens with too much context.
```

### Comment

```text
A or B: which hook is stronger?
Which first frame would you swipe on?
Drop your niche and I’ll rewrite the hook.
Which version is clearer: bad, better, or best?
```

### Follow

```text
Follow for more retention teardowns.
Follow the Caption Lab for the next test.
I’m testing one short-form variable every day. Follow the lab.
Follow if you want the next first-frame breakdown.
```
